name: Fusion VCO2
description: Analog AS3340 VCO through a vacuum tube overdrive. -1 oct transistor sub with Colour low-pass, BBD Detune emulates a second drifting VCO, and a main OUT sums VCO + sub + external audio through the tube.

controls.name: TUNE
controls.detail: ~2-octave knob range · 1V/oct CV tracks over 8+ octaves (C0–C8 and beyond)

controls.name: +1 / -1 OCT switch
controls.description: Three-position octave switch transposing the principal VCO up an octave, leaving it centered, or down an octave.
controls.detail: useful for jumping registers without retuning

controls.name: FM LEVEL
controls.description: Logarithmic attenuator for the external FM CV input before it reaches the exponential frequency control.
controls.detail: exponential FM · CCW: no FM · CW: deep modulation/inharmonic

controls.name: PULSE WIDTH
controls.description: Manual pulse-width control for the internal square waveform that feeds the tube stage and main output.
controls.detail: center: ~50% duty · CCW/CW: asymmetric · also CV-modulatable via PWM IN

controls.name: DETUNE
controls.description: Sets the amount of BBD-delayed, pitch-shifted signal mixed back into the principal oscillator to emulate a second detuned VCO. Also raises the internal LFO that sweeps the BBD clock.
controls.detail: two BBD delay lines acting as a frequency shifter · CCW: off · CW: more detune AND faster internal LFO

controls.name: SUB LEVEL
controls.description: Mix level of the -1 oct transistor sub-oscillator into the main signal path.
controls.detail: CCW: sub off · CW: max sub presence (added on top of the VCO before the tube)

controls.name: COLOUR
controls.description: Switch acting as a fixed low-pass filter on the sub, selecting between two timbre flavours.
controls.detail: two positions · brighter (square-like edge) vs. rounder (filtered, smoother low-end)

controls.name: TUBE CRUNCH
controls.description: Drives the vacuum tube overdrive that processes the sum of VCO + sub + external audio. Manual gain into the dual tube stages.
controls.detail: CCW: clean · noon: warm saturation · CW: aggressive harmonic crunch

controls.name: AUDIO LEVEL
controls.description: Sets how much external audio (from AUDIO IN) is summed into the tube stage alongside the oscillator.
controls.detail: use as a stand-alone tube colour by lowering SUB LEVEL and the VCO's contribution

inputs.name: 1V/OCT
inputs.description: Precision 1V/oct pitch input. Tracks great over 8+ octaves.

inputs.name: FM IN
inputs.description: Exponential FM input attenuated by the FM LEVEL knob. Patch audio-rate sources for classic FM-style tones.

inputs.name: PWM IN
inputs.description: CV input for pulse-width modulation, summed with the PULSE WIDTH knob position.

inputs.name: SYNC IN
inputs.description: Hard-sync input. Rising edges reset the VCO core phase for classic sync sweeps.

inputs.name: AUDIO IN
inputs.description: External audio input summed into the tube overdrive stage alongside the oscillator core. Line-level friendly.

outputs.name: TRI OUT
outputs.description: Dedicated triangle output tapped from the AS3340 core, before the sub mix and the tube stage. Use for clean modulation duties or to feed an external filter.

outputs.name: SUB OUT
outputs.description: Dedicated -1 oct transistor sub output, post-Colour switch, pre-mix.

outputs.name: OUT
outputs.description: Main mix output: principal VCO (square, PWM-shaped) + BBD detune + sub, all driven through the dual tube overdrive. The 'Fusion' voice.

behaviors.name: Tube warm-up
behaviors.description: Vacuum tube needs a brief warm-up after powering the rack. Tone darkens and gain stabilises over the first minute as the heater reaches operating temperature.

behaviors.name: BBD detune as fake-unison
behaviors.description: Two BBD delay lines act as a frequency shifter that returns a pitch-drifted copy of the oscillator. Low settings produce chorusing; higher settings emulate a second detuned VCO without using a second core. The same DETUNE knob also speeds up the internal LFO that sweeps the BBD clock, so detune motion gets faster as you turn CW.

behaviors.name: External audio through the tube
behaviors.description: Any modular or line-level source patched into AUDIO IN is summed with the oscillator before the tube stage. Pull SUB LEVEL and the VCO's contribution down and the module becomes a stand-alone tube overdrive / colour effect.

behaviors.name: PWM CV animation
behaviors.description: Modulating PWM with an LFO or envelope animates the duty cycle of the internal square that feeds the tube stage, producing classic chorus-like timbral movement on the main OUT.

behaviors.name: Hard sync sweeps
behaviors.description: SYNC IN hard-resets the VCO phase on each rising edge. Sweeping TUNE over a synced master produces the classic formant-like sync-lead timbres, amplified and coloured by the tube stage on OUT.

behaviors.name: Sub Colour voicing
behaviors.description: The COLOUR switch is a fixed low-pass filter on the -1 oct sub. One position keeps the sub bright and edgy; the other rounds it off into a smoother low-end reinforcement. Choose per bassline character.

behaviors.name: Octave-switch register jumps
behaviors.description: Hard transposes the principal VCO by an octave up or down without touching TUNE — handy live for jumping the same melody or sequence between bass, mid and lead registers.

behaviors.name: Clean taps via TRI / SUB OUT
behaviors.trigger: patch TRI OUT or SUB OUT
behaviors.description: TRI OUT and SUB OUT bypass the tube stage entirely, giving you clean oscillator material for FM duty, filter input, or layering the dirty OUT against a clean fundamental in the mixer.

patchIdeas.name: Fat tube bass
patchIdeas.patch: Pitch CV → 1V/OCT · -1 OCT down · SUB LEVEL high with a darker COLOUR · TUBE CRUNCH past noon · OUT through a VCA shaped by the gate.
steps: Patch your sequencer's pitch CV → Fusion VCO2 1V/OCT.
Set the +1/-1 OCT switch to -1 OCT for a bass register.
Turn TUNE so the lowest note lands where you want the kick to sit.
Set SUB LEVEL to 3 o'clock and choose the rounder COLOUR position for fat low-end (or the brighter one if you want bite).
Set DETUNE fully CCW (off) to keep the bass tight.
Set PULSE WIDTH around 11 o'clock for a slightly asymmetric square.
Turn TUBE CRUNCH to about 3 o'clock for warm harmonic distortion.
Patch your sequencer's gate → VCA gate input.
Patch Fusion VCO2 OUT → VCA input → mixer.
patchIdeas.signalOut: OUT — the post-tube main mix, ~10 Vptp, gated by the VCA.
patchIdeas.hear: A thick, growling tube bass with a solid sub fundamental and gritty harmonics from the tube stage. Pulling TUBE CRUNCH back gives a rounder, jazzier tone; pushing SUB LEVEL CCW thins it out into more of a mid-bass.
patchIdeas.diagram:
FUSION VCO2:
* +1/-1 OCT: -1 OCT
* SUB LEVEL: 3 o'clock
* COLOUR: rounder pos
* TUBE CRUNCH: 3 o'clock
* DETUNE: off

- Seq (CV) p> Fusion VCO2 (1V/OCT)
- Seq (Gate) g> VCA (Gate)
- Fusion VCO2 (OUT) -> VCA (In)
- VCA (Out) -> Mixer (In)

patchIdeas.name: Detuned unison lead
patchIdeas.patch: Sequencer → 1V/OCT · DETUNE around noon for a 'second VCO' shimmer · PULSE WIDTH animated by a slow LFO · OUT into a filter for screaming leads.
steps: Patch your sequencer's pitch CV → Fusion VCO2 1V/OCT.
Centre the +1/-1 OCT switch (no transpose) and tune TUNE to your lead register.
Set DETUNE to noon — you should hear two oscillators beating against each other.
Patch a slow LFO → Fusion VCO2 PWM IN, set PULSE WIDTH knob to noon for symmetric starting point.
Set TUBE CRUNCH around noon for a warm but musical drive.
Set SUB LEVEL low (8 o'clock) so the sub adds body without dominating.
Patch Fusion VCO2 OUT → filter input.
Patch an envelope → filter cutoff CV for per-note brightness.
Patch filter output → VCA → mixer.
patchIdeas.signalOut: OUT — fat detuned lead, ~10 Vptp, filter-shaped per note.
patchIdeas.hear: A wide, chorused lead voice that sounds like two VCOs running into a tube preamp. The PWM LFO adds slow timbral motion; raising DETUNE further pushes the second 'VCO' into more dramatic frequency-shifter territory.
patchIdeas.diagram:
FUSION VCO2:
* DETUNE: noon
* PULSE WIDTH: noon
* TUBE CRUNCH: noon
* SUB LEVEL: 8 o'clock

- Seq (CV) p> Fusion VCO2 (1V/OCT)
- LFO (Out) >> Fusion VCO2 (PWM IN)
- Fusion VCO2 (OUT) -> VCF (In)
- Env (Out) >> VCF (Cutoff)
- VCF (Out) -> VCA (In)

patchIdeas.name: Slow PWM pad
patchIdeas.patch: Hold a chord on TRI OUT for a clean sine-ish layer, animate the dirty OUT with PWM CV, blend in a touch of DETUNE for shimmer, and send the whole thing to reverb.
steps: Patch your sequencer/keyboard pitch CV → Fusion VCO2 1V/OCT.
Set TUNE for a mid-pad register and centre the +1/-1 OCT switch.
Set PULSE WIDTH to 1 o'clock and patch a very slow LFO (~0.1 Hz) → PWM IN.
Set DETUNE to about 9 o'clock — gentle chorus.
Set SUB LEVEL low and TUBE CRUNCH around 11 o'clock for warmth without crunch.
Patch Fusion VCO2 TRI OUT → Mixer Input 1 (clean fundamental).
Patch Fusion VCO2 OUT → Reverb → Mixer Input 2 (animated body).
Sum and listen — the mix gives a clean carrier with a breathing tube layer behind it.
patchIdeas.signalOut: Mixer output — clean TRI fundamental + reverbed, PWM-animated tube OUT.
patchIdeas.hear: A soft, evolving pad: the TRI provides a clean pitch reference while the PWM-modulated OUT slowly breathes underneath. Raise TUBE CRUNCH for a darker, more saturated pad; raise DETUNE for more chorus motion.
patchIdeas.diagram:
FUSION VCO2:
* PULSE WIDTH: 1 o'clock
* DETUNE: 9 o'clock
* TUBE CRUNCH: 11 o'clock
* SUB LEVEL: low

- Seq (CV) p> Fusion VCO2 (1V/OCT)
- LFO (slow) >> Fusion VCO2 (PWM IN)
- Fusion VCO2 (TRI OUT) -> Mixer (Ch 1)
- Fusion VCO2 (OUT) -> Reverb (In)
- Reverb (Out) -> Mixer (Ch 2)

patchIdeas.name: Sync lead scream
patchIdeas.patch: Master VCO → SYNC IN · envelope → FM IN for per-note FM kick · TUBE CRUNCH cranked · OUT to the amp for searing sync leads.
steps: Patch a master VCO's square or saw → Fusion VCO2 SYNC IN.
Patch your sequencer's pitch CV → both the master VCO 1V/oct and Fusion VCO2 1V/OCT.
Tune Fusion VCO2 TUNE noticeably above the master so you hear sync sidebands.
Patch a fast envelope → Fusion VCO2 FM IN, FM LEVEL to 1 o'clock.
Set TUBE CRUNCH fully CW for screaming harmonics.
Set SUB LEVEL low (3 o'clock or less) so the sub doesn't muddy the sync.
Patch Fusion VCO2 OUT → VCA → amp.
Mult the same envelope → VCA CV for per-note dynamics.
patchIdeas.signalOut: OUT — searing sync lead through the tube, ~10 Vptp.
patchIdeas.hear: Classic hard-sync formants — sweeping TUNE while the master holds steady creates that 'ow!' sync-sweep voice, here amplified and coloured by the tube. Rolling FM LEVEL back tames the FM kick into a softer sync attack.
patchIdeas.diagram:
FUSION VCO2:
* TUBE CRUNCH: full CW
* FM LEVEL: 1 o'clock
* SUB LEVEL: low

- Master VCO (Sqr) -> Fusion VCO2 (SYNC IN)
- Seq (CV) p> Master VCO (1V/oct)
- Seq (CV) p> Fusion VCO2 (1V/OCT)
- Env (Out) >> Fusion VCO2 (FM IN)
- Env (Out) >> VCA (CV)
- Fusion VCO2 (OUT) -> VCA (In)

patchIdeas.name: External audio tube colour
patchIdeas.patch: Drum bus → AUDIO IN · SUB LEVEL down · VCO inaudible (low PWM contribution / TUNE out of register) · TUBE CRUNCH to taste · OUT into the mixer for parallel grit.
steps: Patch a drum bus or any submix → Fusion VCO2 AUDIO IN.
Set SUB LEVEL fully CCW (sub off).
Pull TUNE to an extreme so the VCO is out of the way (or unpatch 1V/OCT and ignore it).
Set the AUDIO LEVEL knob to noon for unity.
Set TUBE CRUNCH to 2 o'clock for a warm, edge-of-breakup tone.
Patch Fusion VCO2 OUT → an aux channel on your mixer.
Blend the parallel tube channel under the dry drums for thickness.
Push TUBE CRUNCH further CW for grungier saturation.
patchIdeas.signalOut: OUT — your external audio re-coloured by the vacuum tube stage, ~10 Vptp.
patchIdeas.hear: Anything you feed in (drums, vocals, a synth submix) takes on the warm, slightly gritty character of the Fusion's tube. Useful for parallel saturation, a 'tape' colour, or full-on overdriven destruction at higher TUBE CRUNCH settings.
patchIdeas.diagram:
FUSION VCO2:
* SUB LEVEL: 0
* TUBE CRUNCH: 2 o'clock
* AUDIO LEVEL: noon

- Drum Bus (Out) -> Fusion VCO2 (AUDIO IN)
- Fusion VCO2 (OUT) -> Mixer (Aux In)

patchIdeas.name: FM bells from TRI
patchIdeas.patch: Modulator VCO → FM IN · FM LEVEL moderate · take TRI OUT (clean, pre-tube) → VCA for bell-like FM tones without saturation.
steps: Patch a modulator VCO's sine or triangle → Fusion VCO2 FM IN.
Set FM LEVEL to noon for a moderate index.
Patch your sequencer's pitch CV → Fusion VCO2 1V/OCT.
Tune the modulator to a non-integer ratio of the carrier (e.g. 1.41×) for inharmonic bells.
Set TUBE CRUNCH fully CCW (clean) — we're using TRI OUT, but keep the rest of the module quiet.
Patch Fusion VCO2 TRI OUT → VCA input (TRI OUT bypasses the tube stage).
Patch a percussive envelope → VCA CV.
Trigger the envelope per note for clean FM bells.
patchIdeas.signalOut: VCA out — clean FM bell tone derived from TRI OUT, no tube colour.
patchIdeas.hear: Crystalline FM bells with the inharmonic spectrum of classic 4-op FM. Switching the modulator to integer ratios gives metallic mallets; raising FM LEVEL pushes into chaotic, gong-like territory.
patchIdeas.diagram:
FUSION VCO2:
* FM LEVEL: noon
* TUBE CRUNCH: 0 (clean)

- Mod VCO (Sine) >> Fusion VCO2 (FM IN)
- Seq (CV) p> Fusion VCO2 (1V/OCT)
- Fusion VCO2 (TRI OUT) -> VCA (In)
- Env (Out) >> VCA (CV)

patchIdeas.name: BBD chorus stack
patchIdeas.patch: Hold one note · DETUNE swept slowly by hand or LFO · TRI OUT and OUT mixed in stereo for a wide chorused drone.
steps: Patch a fixed pitch CV (or just hold a note) → Fusion VCO2 1V/OCT.
Set TUNE to a comfortable mid register, +1/-1 OCT centred.
Set DETUNE around 1 o'clock for clear beating.
Set SUB LEVEL low, TUBE CRUNCH around 11 o'clock for warmth.
Patch Fusion VCO2 TRI OUT → Mixer Left (clean fundamental).
Patch Fusion VCO2 OUT → Mixer Right (detuned, tubed signal).
Optional: patch a very slow LFO → PWM IN for extra timbral shimmer on the OUT side.
Listen in headphones — the channels should beat against each other slowly across the stereo field.
patchIdeas.signalOut: Stereo mixer — TRI OUT (L) clean vs. OUT (R) detuned + tubed.
patchIdeas.hear: A wide stereo chorus drone. The clean TRI on one side stays steady while the BBD-detuned OUT on the other side drifts in and out of tune, producing rich beating and a 'breathing' stereo image. Raise DETUNE for stronger frequency-shifter motion.
patchIdeas.diagram:
FUSION VCO2:
* DETUNE: 1 o'clock
* TUBE CRUNCH: 11 o'clock

- Pitch (CV) p> Fusion VCO2 (1V/OCT)
- Fusion VCO2 (TRI OUT) -> Mixer (L)
- Fusion VCO2 (OUT) -> Mixer (R)
- LFO (slow) >> Fusion VCO2 (PWM IN) // optional shimmer

patchIdeas.name: PWM square with parallel tube grit
patchIdeas.patch: LFO → PWM IN for classic chorus-y PWM · TUBE CRUNCH high · OUT into a filter for fat PWM lead with tube body.
steps: Patch your sequencer's pitch CV → Fusion VCO2 1V/OCT.
Patch a moderate LFO (~3 Hz) → Fusion VCO2 PWM IN.
Set the PULSE WIDTH knob to noon as the LFO centre.
Set TUBE CRUNCH to 2 o'clock for grit.
Set DETUNE off and SUB LEVEL low.
Patch Fusion VCO2 OUT → filter input.
Patch an envelope → filter cutoff for per-note movement.
Patch filter → VCA → mixer.
patchIdeas.signalOut: Filter/VCA output — PWM-modulated tubed lead voice.
patchIdeas.hear: A classic chorused PWM tone that's been pushed through a tube, giving it more midrange weight than a clean PWM. The LFO rate determines the chorus 'wobble'; faster LFO settings move into vibrato-like timbral motion.
patchIdeas.diagram:
FUSION VCO2:
* PULSE WIDTH: noon
* TUBE CRUNCH: 2 o'clock
* DETUNE: off

- LFO (~3Hz) >> Fusion VCO2 (PWM IN)
- Seq (CV) p> Fusion VCO2 (1V/OCT)
- Fusion VCO2 (OUT) -> VCF (In)
- Env (Out) >> VCF (Cutoff)

patchIdeas.name: Drone with slow detune sweep
patchIdeas.patch: Fixed pitch · slow LFO modulating PWM · DETUNE high so the BBD frequency shifter drifts heavily · SUB LEVEL maxed · OUT into reverb.
steps: Patch a fixed pitch CV (or use the keyboard) → Fusion VCO2 1V/OCT.
Set DETUNE to 3 o'clock — heavy BBD detune AND fast internal LFO.
Set SUB LEVEL fully CW with the rounder COLOUR position for big low-end.
Set PULSE WIDTH around noon and patch a slow LFO → PWM IN for breathing.
Set TUBE CRUNCH at noon for warmth.
Patch Fusion VCO2 OUT → reverb → mixer.
Optional: also patch SUB OUT → mixer for a clean sub layer underneath.
patchIdeas.signalOut: OUT through reverb — a thick, drifting tube drone with optional clean sub layer.
patchIdeas.hear: A massive, slowly evolving drone that breathes between in-tune fundamentals and frequency-shifted dissonance as the BBD's internal LFO sweeps. Reverb glues the moving partials into a single immersive bed.
patchIdeas.diagram:
FUSION VCO2:
* DETUNE: 3 o'clock
* SUB LEVEL: max (rounder COLOUR)
* TUBE CRUNCH: noon

- Pitch (CV) p> Fusion VCO2 (1V/OCT)
- LFO (slow) >> Fusion VCO2 (PWM IN)
- Fusion VCO2 (OUT) -> Reverb (In)
- Fusion VCO2 (SUB OUT) -> Mixer (In) // optional clean sub

patchIdeas.name: Kick body with sub reinforcement
patchIdeas.patch: Fast decay envelope → FM IN for the pitch click · -1 OCT switch · SUB LEVEL up · short VCA on OUT for a tube-coloured kick with built-in sub layer.
steps: Set the +1/-1 OCT switch to -1 OCT and TUNE for a low fundamental.
Patch a fast-decay envelope → Fusion VCO2 FM IN, set FM LEVEL to 2 o'clock.
Set SUB LEVEL to about 2 o'clock with the rounder COLOUR position for kick weight.
Set DETUNE off so the kick stays tight.
Set TUBE CRUNCH around 1 o'clock for body and bite.
Patch a trigger → envelope (Trig).
Mult the envelope → VCA CV for the kick amplitude shape.
Patch Fusion VCO2 OUT → VCA → mixer.
patchIdeas.signalOut: VCA out — analog tube kick, pitched by FM IN, with built-in sub layer.
patchIdeas.hear: A thick, club-ready kick. The envelope on FM IN gives the classic pitch-down click on the attack; the sub adds weight; the tube glues it together with harmonic warmth. Tighten TUBE CRUNCH for cleaner kicks; push it for distorted industrial thumps.
patchIdeas.diagram:
FUSION VCO2:
* +1/-1 OCT: -1 OCT
* SUB LEVEL: 2 o'clock
* COLOUR: rounder pos
* TUBE CRUNCH: 1 o'clock
* FM LEVEL: 2 o'clock

- Trig (Out) t> Env (Trig)
- Env (Out) >> Fusion VCO2 (FM IN)
- Env (Out) >> VCA (CV)
- Fusion VCO2 (OUT) -> VCA (In)
